Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Represents a patient using the DigiLab Connect platform.

profileImageUrl.description: URL of the patient's profile image.

googleId.description: Google ID associated with the patient's account (if applicable).

TestRequest.title: TestRequest
TestRequest.description: Represents a test request submitted by a patient.

id.description: Unique identifier for the test request.

symptoms.description: Patient's symptoms related to the test request.

collectionMethod.description: Method of sample collection (home or lab).

labId.description: Reference to Lab. (Relationship: Lab 1:N TestRequest). Optional, if patient has no preference.

status.description: The progress status of the request

supportingFiles.description: List of references to file uploads

Test.title: Test
Test.description: Represents a test that can be requested.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lab owner.

googleId.description: Google ID associated with the lab owner's account (if applicable).

id.description: Unique identifier for the lab personnel.

googleId.description: Google ID associated with the lab personnel's account (if applicable).

TestResult.title: TestResult
TestResult.description: Represents the results of a test.

id.description: Unique identifier for the test result.

resultData.description: The actual test results data (e.g., JSON, text).

uploadDate.description: Date the test results were uploaded.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Represents payment information for a test request.

amount.description: Amount paid for the test request.

paymentMethod.description: Method of payment (e.g., Mpesa, bank transfer).

transactionId.description: Unique transaction identifier from the payment gateway.

accountNumber.description: Unique account number for alternative payment methods.

PersonnelRating.title: PersonnelRating
PersonnelRating.description: Represents a rating given by a patient to lab personnel.

AbuseReport.title: AbuseReport
AbuseReport.description: Represents a report of abuse against lab personnel.

id.description: Unique identifier for the abuse report.

definition.description: Stores patient information. Path-based ownership for easy security rules.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient.

params.name: testId
params.description: The unique identifier of the test.

params.name: labId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lab.

params.name: labOwnerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lab owner.

params.name: labPersonnelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lab personnel.

definition.description: Stores test requests made by patients. Includes denormalized 'patientId' for authorization independence.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient.

params.name: testRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the test request.

definition.description: Stores test results for a specific test request. Includes denormalized 'patientId' and 'testRequestId' for authorization independence.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient.

params.name: testRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the test request.

params.name: testResultId
params.description: The unique identifier of the test result.

definition.description: Stores payment information for a test request. Includes denormalized 'patientId' and 'testRequestId' for authorization independence.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient.

params.name: testRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the test request.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the payment.

definition.description: Stores ratings given by patients to lab personnel. Includes denormalized 'patientId' for authorization independence.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient.

params.name: personnelRatingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the personnel rating.

definition.description: Stores abuse reports submitted by patients. Includes denormalized 'patientId' for authorization independence.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient.

params.name: abuseReportId
params.description: The unique identifier of the abuse report.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. It leverages denormalization to avoid hierarchical authorization dependencies and structural segregation to maintain a homogeneous security posture within collections.

*   **Patients:** Patients are stored under `/patients/{patientId}`. This path-based ownership simplifies security rules. The `googleId` attribute facilitates login via Google.
*   **Tests:** Tests are stored in a flat `/tests/{testId}` collection. This is appropriate as tests are global and not owned by individual users.
*   **Labs:** Labs are stored under `/labs/{labId}`. The `ownerId` attribute establishes the relationship between labs and lab owners. The root-level collection simplifies retrieval.
*   **Lab Owners:** Lab owners are stored under `/lab_owners/{labOwnerId}`.  The `googleId` attribute facilitates login via Google.
*   **Lab Personnel:** Lab personnel are stored under `/lab_personnel/{labPersonnelId}`. The `labId` attribute links personnel to labs. This is stored in a root-level collection simplifying personnel retrieval.
*   **Test Requests:** Test requests are stored under `/patients/{patientId}/test_requests/{testRequestId}`. This reflects the 1:N relationship between patients and test requests.  To support authorization independence, we denormalize a `patientId` field inside the test request. This allows security rules to validate that the authenticated user `request.auth.uid` matches the `patientId` in the test request without needing to read the parent patient document.
*   **Test Results:** Test results are stored under `/patients/{patientId}/test_requests/{testRequestId}/test_results/{testResultId}`. This nested structure reflects the relationship between patients, test requests, and test results. Again, to enforce authorization independence, `patientId` and `testRequestId` are denormalized into each `test_results` document.
*   **Payments:** Payments are stored under `/patients/{patientId}/test_requests/{testRequestId}/payments/{paymentId}`. This reflects the link between a patient, a test request and its payment. `patientId` and `testRequestId` are denormalized in each `payments` document for authorization independence.
*   **Personnel Ratings:** Personnel ratings are stored under `/patients/{patientId}/personnel_ratings/{personnelRatingId}`.  We denormalize `patientId` into each `personnel_ratings` document.
*   **Abuse Reports:** Abuse reports are stored under `/patients/{patientId}/abuse_reports/{abuseReportId}`. We denormalize `patientId` into each `abuse_reports` document.

This structure supports QAPs by:

*   **Enabling secure list operations:**  Path-based ownership and denormalization allow listing of test requests, results, payments, ratings, and reports securely, as rules can efficiently filter based on the authenticated user's ID without needing complex `get()` calls.
*   **Maintaining Invariants:** The structure supports the integrity of ownership, timestamps, and denormalized data. Security rules can enforce that `patientId` in subcollections always matches the parent patient ID and that timestamps are immutable after creation.
